ogTitle: West Footscray vs West Wodonga: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Footscray and West Wodonga.
heading: West Footscray vs West Wodonga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Footscray, Victoria and West Wodonga, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Footscray has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to West Wodonga (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Footscray or West Wodonga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Footscray has a flood risk score of 30/100, while West Wodonga scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Footscray scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. West Wodonga scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Footscray or West Wodonga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Footscray has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
